familySearch.familiesToTrainDescription: Families that still need FamilySearch training.
familySearch.faq.a1: FamilySearch helps families discover, gather, and unite their family through family history and temple ordinances. Training members increases participation and strengthens eternal family bonds.
familySearch.faq.a2: Prioritize those who are interested, have device access, and time to practice—especially new members and those who have not started a family tree.
familySearch.faq.a3: Creating or signing into an account, adding close relatives, searching records, saving sources, and when appropriate reserving temple ordinances with care and accuracy.
familySearch.faq.a4: Mark the family as trained, leave a note about what they learned, and schedule a short follow-up to answer questions and encourage weekly progress.
familySearch.faq.q2: Whom should we prioritize for training?

missionaryWork.assignments.description: Tasks and responsibilities to support the work in the ward.
missionaryWork.assignments.title: Missionary Assignments
missionaryWork.description: Coordinate and track your organization's missionary efforts.
missionaryWork.faq.a1: Your organization, under the direction of the bishop, leads missionary work in the ward. Their main responsibility is to coordinate members' efforts to find, teach, and baptize interested individuals. This includes working closely with full-time missionaries, organizing missionary activities, and ensuring new converts are integrated and supported.
missionaryWork.faq.a2: Collaboration is key. The organization presidency should meet regularly with the missionaries in missionary correlation meetings to coordinate plans. Organization members can help missionaries by providing referrals, participating in lessons, offering transportation, and opening their homes for activities.
missionaryWork.faq.a3: Assigning friends (often called "ministering brothers" or simply organization friends) is crucial for retaining new members. A new convert needs support, friendship, and guidance. Assigning one or two organization members to befriend the new member, visit them, invite them to activities, and answer their questions helps them feel part of the community and strengthens their testimony.
missionaryWork.faq.a4: Assignments can be varied: accompanying the missionaries to a lesson, inviting a friend to a Church activity, sharing a Gospel message on social media, helping an investigator with a move as an act of service, or organizing a home evening open to friends of the Church.
missionaryWork.faq.description: Answers to common questions about your organization's role in missionary work.
missionaryWork.faq.q1: What is the role of your organization in missionary work?
missionaryWork.faq.q2: How do we work with full-time missionaries?
missionaryWork.faq.q3: What does it mean to "assign friends" to a new convert, and why is it important?
missionaryWork.faq.q4: What kind of missionary assignments can your organization have?
missionaryWork.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
